cpu: AMD A8-3850 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Current TS20xx build 66.1b [1.] Unzip all .ap files in Assets & Route folders with 7zip [2.] Replace OpenAL32.dll with the one in the Install/OpenAL/64bit folder to play TS in 64-bit. {... so it CANNOT be suddenly "platinum' perfect, but works afterwards as stated before} I still think the performance is sub-par compared to Win7/10. Also graphic tweaks (ReShade or Nvidia 3D Vision) won't work.
title: Train Simulator 2019

notes: I only have the absolute base game, no mods add-ons, DLCs or anything. So for me, I had to do nothing to get the game started and running. Other people describe unpacking files, but I did not have to do this. Your mileage may vary though. The gameplay is very similar to the game on Windows, although I would say that the FPS is lower, but it is more consistent than on Windows. When I've played this game on Windows it stutters and I get large framerate drops. On Manjaro it runs much more consistent so that's good. I would tweak the graphics settings to a bit lower, but that's about it. Train stations and other trains passing lower the FPS temporarily too.
